config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menindas orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi “demi kepentingan bersama” untuk dipakai membenarkan ketidakadilan, jadi prioritas pada hak individu itu penting sebagai pelindung. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support giving society blanket priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach, discrimination, and pressure on minorities to just “go along.” At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and basic order do require some limits, but those limits need to be narrow, accountable, and justified. To me, the burden should be on society to prove why a restriction on a person’s rights is truly necessary, not on the individual to prove why they deserve protection. If we reverse that presumption, it becomes too easy for the majority to silence unpopular people in the name of the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pemaksaan, stigma, atau menekan kelompok kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau negara membatasi diri dan hanya membatasi hak individu saat benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi kedok untuk ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take blanket priority over the individual, because in the United States that logic has repeatedly been used to excuse major abuses of power and to pressure minorities to stay quiet. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; real public needs like safety, disease control, and basic civic order can justify some restrictions when they are specific and tightly accountable. What I reject is the idea that the “common good” should automatically outrank a person’s rights, because that makes it too easy for the majority to trample unpopular people. In my view, the default should be the other way around: protect the individual first, and force society to make a very strong case before limiting that protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, slogan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi alasan untuk memaksa warga kecil, menekan minoritas, atau menutup mata terhadap penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah menjadi alat untuk membungkam orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society above the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often justified overreach and mistreatment of unpopular groups. Public safety and order do matter, but they should be the exception, not the default excuse, and any limit on rights should be narrow, evidence-based, and easy to challenge. I also do not buy the idea that the majority’s sense of the common good is automatically more legitimate than an individual’s rights, because majorities can be wrong and can be cruel. To me, a healthy society protects people first and only restricts rights when it can prove that doing so is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
